description_en: Relating to education and scholarship.
example: The school has very high academic standards.

description_en: The subjects comprising a course of study in a school or college.
example: The history curriculum covers ancient civilizations.

description_en: The method and practice of teaching, especially as an academic subject or theoretical concept.
example: Innovation in pedagogy is necessary for modern students.

meaning: biết chữ; trình độ học vấn
description_en: The ability to read and write.
example: The government is working to improve adult literacy rates.
note: Can also refer to knowledge in a specific area (e.g., digital literacy).

description_en: A grant or payment made to support a student's education, awarded on the basis of academic or other achievement.
example: She won a full scholarship to study at Harvard.

description_en: Sum of money charged for teaching or instruction by a school, college, or university.
example: The university increased its tuition fees this year.

description_en: A task or piece of work assigned to someone as part of a job or course of study.
example: The math assignment is due next Tuesday.
note: Commonly used in schools and workplaces.

description_en: The practice of training people to obey rules or a code of behavior.
example: Sociology is a relatively young academic discipline.
note: Refers to both behavior and fields of study.

description_en: The action of enrolling or being enrolled.
example: Course enrollment opens online next Monday.
note: The process of joining a course or school.

description_en: The evaluation or estimation of the nature, quality, or ability of someone or something.
example: Periodic assessment helps track student progress.

description_en: A person who has successfully completed a course of study or training.
example: She is a graduate of Oxford University.
note: Can be a noun or a verb.

description_en: A person who gives lectures, especially as an occupation at a university or college.
example: The guest lecturer spoke about environmental policy.

meaning: bản cáo bạch; giới thiệu trường
description_en: A printed booklet advertising a school or university.
example: You should check the university prospectus for course details.

description_en: The topics in a course of study or teaching.
example: The syllabus lists all the required readings for the semester.

description_en: A long essay or dissertation involving personal research, written by a candidate for a college degree.
example: He is writing his PhD thesis on artificial intelligence.
note: Major research project for advanced degrees.

description_en: Relating to an occupation or employment.
example: The college offers vocational training in automotive repair.
note: Focuses on practical skills for jobs.

description_en: A meeting at which a group of people engage in intensive discussion and activity on a particular subject or project.
example: They attended a creative writing workshop.

description_en: The action or state of going regularly to or being present at a place or event.
example: Attendance is mandatory for all lab sessions.

description_en: A certificate awarded by an educational establishment to show that someone has successfully completed a course of study.
example: He received his high school diploma last June.

description_en: The teaching staff of a university or college.
example: The law faculty is known for its distinguished professors.
note: Refers to both the staff and a department.

description_en: Learning or literature concerned with human culture.
example: History and philosophy are part of the humanities.
note: Study of human society and culture.

description_en: The position of a student or trainee who works in an organization, sometimes without pay, in order to gain work experience.
example: She did a summer internship at a law firm.

description_en: Required by law or rules; compulsory.
example: The introductory course is mandatory for all first-year students.

description_en: A program of introduction for newcomers to a university or other institution.
example: Student orientation starts a week before classes begin.
note: Helps students acclimate to campus life.

description_en: The practice of taking someone else's work or ideas and passing them off as one's own.
example: Plagiarism is a serious academic offense.
note: Ethical and legal violation in research and writing.

description_en: A thing that is required as a prior condition for something else to happen or exist.
example: Biology 101 is a prerequisite for the advanced genetics course.
note: Must be completed before taking a higher-level course.

meaning: phòng đào tạo; người đăng ký
description_en: An official responsible for keeping a register or official records.
example: Contact the registrar's office to request a transcript.

description_en: A half-year term in a school or college, typically lasting fifteen to eighteen weeks.
example: The fall semester begins in late August.
note: Common division of the academic year.

description_en: A conference or other meeting for discussion or training.
example: The professor held a small seminar for his graduate students.

description_en: A university student who has not yet taken a first degree.
example: The undergraduate program takes four years to complete.
note: Student working towards a bachelor's degree.

description_en: The action of checking or proving the validity or accuracy of something.
example: The study needs further validation through controlled experiments.
note: Proving the correctness of research results.

description_en: Former students or graduates of a particular school, college, or university.
example: The alumni association organizes yearly reunions.

description_en: The action or process of providing someone or something with an official document attesting to a status or level of achievement.
example: He received certification in project management.

description_en: The mental action or process of acquiring knowledge and understanding through thought, experience, and the senses.
example: The course explores human cognition and memory.

description_en: The ability to do something successfully or efficiently.
example: The exam tests your technical competence in the field.

description_en: A qualification, achievement, personal quality, or aspect of a person's background, typically used to indicate that they are suitable for something.
example: Does he have the right credentials for the teaching position?

description_en: Put off (an action or event) to a later time; postpone.
example: She deferred her admission to the university for a year.
note: Often used for Gap years or taking time off.

description_en: A long essay on a particular subject, especially one written as a requirement for the award of a university degree.
example: He defended his doctoral dissertation in May.

description_en: Optional course of study.
example: She chose photography as an elective this semester.

description_en: Pursued in addition to the normal course of study.
example: Playing sports is a popular extracurricular activity.

description_en: The base on which something is built.
example: The course provides a strong foundation in calculus.

description_en: A thing that motivates or encourages someone to do something.
example: The university offers financial incentives for researchers.

description_en: Relating to more than one branch of knowledge.
example: The program is interdisciplinary, combining art and science.

description_en: The guidance provided by a mentor, especially an experienced person in a company or educational institution.
example: The mentorship program pairs students with industry professionals.

description_en: A thing aimed at or sought; a goal.
example: What are the learning objectives of this module?

description_en: A high degree of skill; expertise.
example: The job requires proficiency in at least two languages.

description_en: The action of providing source information for facts and ideas used in a text.
example: Correct referencing is essential to avoid plagiarism.
note: Identifying sources in academic writing (e.g., APA, MLA).

description_en: A scoring guide used to evaluate the quality of students' constructed responses.
example: The professor handed out a rubric for the final project.
note: Defines what's expected for each grade layer.

description_en: An official record of a student's work, showing courses taken and grades achieved.
example: The employer asked for an official university transcript.

description_en: A strong feeling of suitability for a particular career or occupation.
example: He felt a vocation for nursing from a young age.
note: Often implies a calling or deep passion for a job.
